basics.name: Yash Ganatra
basics.label: Thermal Engineer
basics.summary: Thermal engineer who ships consumer electronics. 8+ years taking products from first concept through mass production — thermal validation (IR imaging, thermocouples, automated test frameworks), thermal simulation, evaluation of thermal solutions, and software thermal management policies that balance performance with thermal comfort and safety.

work.summary: Thermal testing and design of iPad Air (6th & 7th gen, 2024/2025) and iPhone 17 (2025).
highlights: Own all thermal objectives from initial concept through launch, defining software thermal management policies that balance performance and thermal comfort
Characterize thermal performance across real-world use cases — benchmarks, gaming, video streaming, charging, camera — and analyze field metrics to mirror customer usage in tests
Define touch-temperature limits through user perception studies; design virtual temperature sensors that predict skin hotspots and cut test time
Evaluate feasibility and performance of thermal solutions: graphite spreaders, graphite-wrapped foams, copper sheets and TIMs
Partner with power teams to define and validate use-case power budgets, driving EE optimizations when power exceeds targets
Commissioned a thermal validation lab at a vendor site and authored thermal test SOPs

work.summary: Thermal testing and design of Echo Dot (3rd & 5th gen), Echo (3rd gen), Echo Show 10, Echo Studio, Echo Flex, Halo Rise and the Astro home robot.
highlights: Enabled $250k cost savings for Echo Dot by validating and assessing thermal risk during new product development
Led thermal validation of Amazon's first consumer robot (Astro) against TS-1/TS-2 thermal safety specs
Developed virtual temperature sensors to predict skin hotspots across safety validation tests, reducing test time
Built a Python test-automation framework that cut test time by 50% by automating music/video streaming, display and LED control, and internal temperature and power capture
Developed the Temperature Sensor feature on Echo Dot (5th gen) by collecting and processing large-scale device data with Python and Unix scripting

work.summary: Developed portable temperature measurement systems from concept to manufacturing for the Iron & Steel industry.
highlights: Standardized thermocouple calibration; tested and troubleshot hardware and software prototypes
Sped up product builds by 25% with MATLAB algorithms for processing field data
Trained distributors and sales teams; presented at AFS and AIST industry conferences

work.summary: Master's thesis: cooling smartphones using Phase Change Materials.
highlights: Developed and validated a passive cooling solution (COMSOL, Ansys Fluent, IR camera, thermocouples), cutting processor temperatures by 5%
Published in the International Journal of Thermal Sciences; presented at ASME InterPACK

volunteer.summary: Make learning fun and more relatable
highlights: Facilitate better understanding of energy conversion through hands on experiments and examples

publications.name: Experimental investigation of Phase Change Materials for thermal management of handheld devices
publications.summary: Hardware thermal testing to quantify usefulness of PCMs in cooling smartphones.

publications.name: Investigation of aluminum foams and graphite fillers for improving the thermal conductivity of paraffin wax-based phase change materials
publications.summary: Improving effective thermal conductivity of PCM-based passive cooling.

publications.name: Passive Thermal Management Using Phase Change Materials: Experimental Evaluation of Thermal Resistances
publications.summary: Characterize thermal resistance of PCMs using IR camera and reference bar method (ASTM D5470 standard).

skills.name: Thermal Engineering
keywords: Thermal solutions: graphite spreaders, foams, TIMs

interests.name: Outside work
keywords: Built a BAJA SAE off-road vehicle
